24	// fakeExecuteBeadGit is a mock implementation of executeBeadGitOps for tests.
25	type fakeExecuteBeadGit struct {
26		mu sync.Mutex
27	
28		// mainHeadRev is returned by HeadRev/ResolveRev for the main working dir.
29		mainHeadRev string
30		// headRevSeq, when set, is returned in order for successive main-dir HeadRev calls.
31		headRevSeq []string
32		headRevIdx int
33		// wtHeadRev is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths (after agent run).
34		wtHeadRev string
35		// wtDirty is returned by IsDirty for worktree paths.
36		wtDirty bool
37		// synthRev, if set, is applied as wtHeadRev when SynthesizeCommit is called.
38		synthRev string
39		// wtHeadRevErr, if set, is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths.
40		wtHeadRevErr error
41		dirty        bool
42		mergeErr     error
43		updateRefErr error
44	
45		addedWTs   []string
46		addedWTRev string
47		removedWTs []string
48		refs       map[string]string // ref -> sha recorded by UpdateRef
49		worktrees  []string          // paths returned by WorktreeList
50	
51		mergeCalls int
52		mergeRev   string
53	}

55	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
56		f.mu.Lock()
57		defer f.mu.Unlock()
58		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
59			if f.wtHeadRevErr != nil {
60				return "", f.wtHeadRevErr
61			}
62			return f.wtHeadRev, nil
63		}
64		if len(f.headRevSeq) > 0 {
65			idx := f.headRevIdx
66			if idx >= len(f.headRevSeq) {
67				idx = len(f.headRevSeq) - 1
68			}
69			rev := f.headRevSeq[idx]
70			f.headRevIdx++
71			return rev, nil
72		}
73		return f.mainHeadRev, nil
74	}
75	
76	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {
77		f.mu.Lock()
78		defer f.mu.Unlock()
79		return f.mainHeadRev, nil
80	}

83		f.mu.Lock()
84		defer f.mu.Unlock()
85		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
86			return f.wtDirty, nil
87		}
88		return f.dirty, nil

93		defer f.mu.Unlock()

95		f.addedWTRev = rev

101			if err := copyTestFile(beadFile, filepath.Join(wtPath, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl")); err != nil {

131		f.mu.Lock()
132		defer f.mu.Unlock()

135			return true, nil
136		}

173	// fakeAgentRunner is a minimal mock agent runner for execute-bead tests.
174	type fakeAgentRunner struct {
175		result *agent.Result
176		err    error
177		last   agent.RunOptions
178		// sideEffect, when set, runs while the runner has the opts in hand. It
179		// is used to simulate runtime state the embedded agent harness would
180		// otherwise write (session logs, telemetry, etc.) so tests can assert
181		// where those files land.
182		sideEffect func(opts agent.RunOptions) error
183	}
184	
185	func (r *fakeAgentRunner) Run(opts agent.RunOptions) (*agent.Result, error) {
186		r.last = opts
187		if r.sideEffect != nil {
188			if err := r.sideEffect(opts); err != nil {
189				return nil, err
190			}
191		}
192		return r.result, r.err
193	}

195	// newExecuteBeadFactory builds a CommandFactory wired with the given fake git and agent runner.
196	func newExecuteBeadFactory(t *testing.T, git *fakeExecuteBeadGit, runner *fakeAgentRunner) *CommandFactory {
197		t.Helper()
198		f := NewCommandFactory(t.TempDir())
199		seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, f.WorkingDir)
200		f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
201		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
202		f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride = git
203		f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride = fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git)
204		return f
205	}

207	// fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit returns a LandingAdvancer callback that maps the
208	// fake git's Merge/UpdateRef semantics onto the coordinator-pattern advancer
209	// interface. Semantically, "mergeCalls" now means "number of times
210	// LandBeadResult invoked the advancer (attempted to advance the target
211	// branch)". Used by tests that were written against the old Merge() path so
212	// they continue to pass after the land-coordinator refactor.
213	func fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git *fakeExecuteBeadGit) func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
214		return func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
215			if err := git.Merge("", res.ResultRev); err != nil {
216				preserveRef := agent.PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
217				// Record the preserve ref in the fake's refs map so tests that
218				// assert git.refs[preserveRef] continue to pass.
219				_ = git.UpdateRef("", preserveRef, res.ResultRev)
220				return &agent.LandResult{
221					Status:      "preserved",
222					PreserveRef: preserveRef,
223					Reason:      "merge failed",
224				}, nil
225			}
226			return &agent.LandResult{Status: "landed", NewTip: res.ResultRev}, nil
227		}
228	}

230	func assertPreserveRef(t *testing.T, ref, beadID, baseRev string) {
231		t.Helper()
232		shortSHA := baseRev
233		if len(shortSHA) > 12 {
234			shortSHA = shortSHA[:12]
235		}
236		pattern := fmt.Sprintf(`^refs/ddx/iterations/%s/\d{8}T\d{6}Z-%s$`,
237			regexp.QuoteMeta(beadID), regexp.QuoteMeta(shortSHA))
238		require.Regexp(t, pattern, ref)
239	}

241	// runExecuteBead invokes the execute-bead command through the cobra tree and returns
242	// the parsed JSON result. It extracts the JSON object from the combined output,
243	// skipping any leading note/warning lines written to stderr.
244	func runExecuteBead(t *testing.T, f *CommandFactory, git *fakeExecuteBeadGit, beadID string, extraArgs ...string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
245		t.Helper()
246		root := f.NewRootCommand()
247		args := append([]string{"agent", "execute-bead", beadID, "--json"}, extraArgs...)
248		out, err := executeCommand(root, args...)
249		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
250		return parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
251	}

253	func parseExecuteBeadJSON(t *testing.T, out string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
254		t.Helper()
255		// Strip any non-JSON prefix lines (e.g. stderr notes written to the shared buffer).
256		jsonStart := strings.Index(out, "{")
257		require.NotEqual(t, -1, jsonStart, "output should contain JSON: %s", out)
258		jsonPart := out[jsonStart:]
259		var res agent.ExecuteBeadResult
260		dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewBufferString(jsonPart))
261		require.NoError(t, dec.Decode(&res), "output should be valid JSON: %s", jsonPart)
262		return res
263	}

293	func copyTree(src, dst string) error {
294		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
295			if err != nil {
296				return err
297			}
298			rel, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
299			if err != nil {
300				return err
301			}
302			target := filepath.Join(dst, rel)
303			if info.IsDir() {
304				return os.MkdirAll(target, info.Mode())
305			}
306			if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0o755); err != nil {
307				return err
308			}
309			in, err := os.Open(path)
310			if err != nil {
311				return err
312			}
313			defer in.Close()
314			out, err := os.Create(target)
315			if err != nil {
316				return err
317			}
318			if _, err := io.Copy(out, in); err != nil {
319				out.Close()
320				return err
321			}
322			if err := out.Close(); err != nil {
323				return err
324			}
325			return os.Chmod(target, info.Mode())
326		})
327	}

356	// TestExecuteBeadMerge verifies that when the agent produces a real commit and
357	// the target branch can advance, the outcome is "merged". Exercises real git
358	// plumbing (worktree add/remove, ref update, file sync) and the script harness
359	// driving an actual commit — no fakes for the components under test.
360	func TestExecuteBeadMerge(t *testing.T) {
361		workDir := t.TempDir()
362	
363		scrubEnv := func() []string {
364			parent := os.Environ()
365			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
366			for _, kv := range parent {
367				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
368					continue
369				}
370				env = append(env, kv)
371			}
372			return env
373		}
374		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
375			t.Helper()
376			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
377			cmd.Dir = workDir
378			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
379			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
380			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
381			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
382		}
383	
384		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
385		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
386		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
387		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
388		runGit("add", ".")
389		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
390	
391		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
392			ID:        "my-bead",
393			Title:     "Test execute-bead merge",
394			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
395			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
396		})
397		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
398		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
399		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
400	
401		// Directive file drives the script harness to make one real commit in the
402		// worker worktree, replacing fakeAgentRunner's canned Result struct.
403		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
404		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
405			"create-file hello.txt hello-content\n"+
406				"commit feat: add hello\n",
407		), 0o644))
408	
409		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
410		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
411		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
412	
413		root := f.NewRootCommand()
414		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
415			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
416		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
417		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
418	
419		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
420		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
421		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
422		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
423		assert.NotEqual(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev)
424		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
425		assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
426		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
427	
428		// Real-git assertion: main must have advanced to ResultRev via Land().
429		assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD"),
430			"main should advance to ResultRev after a successful merge")
431	
432		// The worker worktree must have been created and cleaned up: no entries
433		// under the execute-bead prefix remain in `git worktree list`.
434		wtList := runGit("worktree", "list", "--porcelain")
435		assert.NotContains(t, wtList, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"my-bead-",
436			"execute-bead worktree should be removed after the run")
437	
438		// File from the agent's commit must be materialized on main through real
439		// merge/ff semantics (regression guard for ddx-eaebaffb: update-ref
440		// bypassing the working tree).
441		content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "hello.txt"))
442		require.NoError(t, err, "hello.txt should materialize on main after Land()")
443		assert.Equal(t, "hello-content", string(content))
444	}

446	// TestExecuteBeadPreserveOnMergeFailure verifies that when merge fails
447	// the result is preserved under a hidden ref.
448	func TestExecuteBeadPreserveOnMergeFailure(t *testing.T) {
449		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
450			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
451			wtHeadRev:   "cccc3333",
452			mergeErr:    fmt.Errorf("merge conflict"),
453		}
454		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
455		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
456	
457		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
458	
459		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
460		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
461		assert.Equal(t, "cccc3333", res.ResultRev)
462		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
463		assertPreserveRef(t, res.PreserveRef, "my-bead", "aaaa1111")
464		assert.Equal(t, "merge failed", res.Reason)
465	
466		// Hidden ref should be recorded in the mock.
467		require.Contains(t, git.refs, res.PreserveRef)
468		assert.Equal(t, "cccc3333", git.refs[res.PreserveRef])
469		assert.Equal(t, 1, git.mergeCalls)
470		assert.Equal(t, "cccc3333", git.mergeRev)
471	}

497	// TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness verifies that two runs on the same bead-id
498	// produce distinct preserve refs (concurrent hidden-ref uniqueness).
499	func TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness(t *testing.T) {
500		makeRun := func(ts time.Time) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
501			oldNow := agent.NowFunc
502			agent.NowFunc = func() time.Time { return ts }
503			defer func() { agent.NowFunc = oldNow }()
504	
505			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
506				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
507				wtHeadRev:   "eeee5555",
508				mergeErr:    fmt.Errorf("diverged"),
509			}
510			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
511			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
512			return runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "shared-bead")
513		}
514	
515		res1 := makeRun(time.Date(2026, 4, 10, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC))
516		res2 := makeRun(time.Date(2026, 4, 10, 0, 0, 1, 0, time.UTC))
517	
518		assert.NotEqual(t, res1.PreserveRef, res2.PreserveRef,
519			"concurrent runs must produce distinct preserve refs")
520		assertPreserveRef(t, res1.PreserveRef, "shared-bead", "aaaa1111")
521		assertPreserveRef(t, res2.PreserveRef, "shared-bead", "aaaa1111")
522	}

544	// left uncommitted by the agent are synthesized into a commit and treated as

1193	// TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected verifies that when execute-bead
1194	// invokes the embedded-agent harness, its session/telemetry runtime state is
1195	// redirected into a DDx-owned directory inside the execution bundle instead
1196	// of being written at the worktree root. Regression guard for ddx-cba2dc64.
1197	func TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected(t *testing.T) {
1198		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1199			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111cafe",
1200			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222beef",
1201		}
1202	
1203		// Snapshot of wtPath root contents captured during Run so the assertion
1204		// can run before ExecuteBead removes the worktree on return.
1205		var wtPathDuringRun string
1206		var wtRootBefore []string
1207		var wtRootAfter []string
1208		var sessionLogDirSeen string
1209		simulatedSessionFile := "agent-embedded-session.jsonl"
1210	
1211		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{
1212			result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "agent"},
1213			sideEffect: func(opts agent.RunOptions) error {
1214				wtPathDuringRun = opts.WorkDir
1215				sessionLogDirSeen = opts.SessionLogDir
1216	
1217				// Capture worktree root listing before simulating writes.
1218				entries, err := os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
1219				if err != nil {
1220					return err
1221				}
1222				for _, e := range entries {
1223					wtRootBefore = append(wtRootBefore, e.Name())
1224				}
1225	
1226				// Simulate the embedded-agent harness writing runtime state.
1227				// It MUST land in opts.SessionLogDir, not opts.WorkDir. If the
1228				// execute-bead wiring is broken and SessionLogDir is empty or the
1229				// worktree root, this write will land at the worktree root and
1230				// the post-check below will catch it.
1231				if opts.SessionLogDir == "" {
1232					return fmt.Errorf("embedded agent runner received empty SessionLogDir; runtime state would land at worktree root")
1233				}
1234				if err := os.MkdirAll(opts.SessionLogDir, 0o755); err != nil {
1235					return err
1236				}
1237				if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(opts.SessionLogDir, simulatedSessionFile), []byte(`{"event":"started"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
1238					return err
1239				}
1240	
1241				// Capture worktree root listing after simulated writes.
1242				entries, err = os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
1243				if err != nil {
1244					return err
1245				}
1246				for _, e := range entries {
1247					wtRootAfter = append(wtRootAfter, e.Name())
1248				}
1249				return nil
1250			},
1251		}
1252		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1253	
1254		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead", "--harness", "agent")
1255		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome, "execute-bead should succeed for this test")
1256	
1257		// The runner must have received a SessionLogDir override.
1258		require.NotEmpty(t, sessionLogDirSeen, "execute-bead must pass a SessionLogDir to the embedded harness")
1259	
1260		// The override must point inside the tracked execution bundle, not at
1261		// the worktree root.
1262		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ExecutionDir, "execute-bead must record an execution bundle dir")
1263		bundleAbs := filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, filepath.FromSlash(res.ExecutionDir))
1264		absLog, err := filepath.Abs(sessionLogDirSeen)
1265		require.NoError(t, err)
1266		absBundle, err := filepath.Abs(bundleAbs)
1267		require.NoError(t, err)
1268		assert.Truef(t, strings.HasPrefix(absLog, absBundle+string(filepath.Separator)),
1269			"SessionLogDir (%s) must be inside the execution bundle (%s)", absLog, absBundle)
1270		assert.Equal(t, "embedded", filepath.Base(absLog),
1271			"SessionLogDir must be the bundle's embedded/ subdirectory")
1272	
1273		// The worktree root must not gain any files during the run.
1274		require.NotEmpty(t, wtPathDuringRun)
1275		assert.Equal(t, wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter,
1276			"worktree root entries must not change while the embedded harness runs (before=%v after=%v)",
1277			wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter)
1278		assert.NotContains(t, wtRootAfter, simulatedSessionFile,
1279			"simulated embedded session file must not land at the worktree root")
1280		assert.NotContains(t, wtRootAfter, ".agent-session.json",
1281			"embedded agent must not write .agent-session.json at the worktree root")
1282	
1283		// The simulated session file must exist at the redirected location.
1284		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(sessionLogDirSeen, simulatedSessionFile))
1285	}

22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}

64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}

125	
126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)
131	
132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}
146	
147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}
